query: mixed color temperature sodium vapor orange streetlight clashing fluorescent night urban
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: mixed color temp — sodium vapor orange vs blue twilight, clashing artificial light sources, desolate urban night

response.objective_caption: A ground-level perspective looking down a narrow urban alleyway at night. A series of overhead streetlamps cast light along the wet asphalt and concrete, creating reflective patches that recede toward a distant vanishing point. The left wall features graffiti tags below a dark window opening, while utility poles and overhead wires run along the upper left. Doors and windows line both sides of the brick and concrete corridor. A lone figure walks in the distance near the center of the passage. Large dumpsters sit against the right wall, and the upper portions of the surrounding buildings are obscured by darkness.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A ground-level perspective looking down a narrow urban alleyway at night, bathed in a sickly orange sodium-vapor cast that shifts walls and pavement toward an unsettling, contaminated hue. Overhead streetlamps create reflective pools on the wet ground while extreme underexposure crushes the side walls, upper stories, and foreground edges into oppressive black voids with no recoverable detail. Graffiti, stains, and deteriorating surfaces mark the decaying environment, evoking a sense of neglect and entropy. A lone figure walks in the distance beneath the receding lights, reinforcing the atmospheric distress and unease of the deteriorating corridor.
